title: CRISPR Editing of Immortalized Cell Lines with RNPs using Nucleofection
author: Synthego
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.spnedme

steps.title: Subculture cells 2 days before nucleofection...
steps.instruction: Subculture cells 2 days before nucleofection and seed cells in an appropriately sized vessel so that they are 70-80% confluent on the day of transfection. Each nucleofection reaction will require ~1.5 x 10 5 cells. Culturing cells for additional days may be necessary to reach the desired confluency. 48:00:00, Subculturing cells

steps.title: Pre-warm 1 ml of normal growth...
steps.instruction: Pre-warm 1 ml of normal growth medium in each well of a 12-well cell culture plate per reaction. undetermined, normal growth medium This will serve as the destination plate after nucleofection.

steps.title: In appropriate plates/tubes, assemble RNP complexes...
steps.instruction: In appropriate plates/tubes, assemble RNP complexes in the order shown below. Synthego recommends sgRNA:Cas9 ratios between 3:1 and 9:1 for RNP formation. Below is an example experiment using a sgRNA:Cas9 ratio of 9:1. Knock-in Option : to knock in small inserts (<50 bp), an ssDNA HDR Template can be added. The recommended length of each homology arm is at least 50 bp. Add 1 µl 60 µM ssDNA HDR Template per reaction to each well. Optimization may be required. To knock in larger inserts and for more information on designing knock-in experiments, see Tips and Tricks: Design and Optimization of CRISPR Knock-in Experiments.

steps.title: For suspension cells
steps.instruction: For suspension cells: spin down cells before each aspiration of culture medium and washes (step 5). Skip steps 6 and 7 below. Aspirate cell culture medium and wash cells 1-2 times with appropriate volume of 1X concentration of PBS. Do not shake or hit the flask to dislodge cells, as this may lead to clumping and inaccuracies in cell counting.

steps.title: Add appropriate amount of TrypLE Express...
steps.instruction: Add appropriate amount of TrypLE Express and incubate the cells for ~5 minutes, or until they detach from the plate completely. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Neutralize the dissociation reaction with at...
steps.instruction: Neutralize the dissociation reaction with at least 2X volume of normal growth medium.

steps.title: Count the cells to determine the cell density
steps.instruction: Count the cells to determine the cell density.

steps.title: Aliquot enough cells to have 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot enough cells to have 1.5 x 10 5 cells/reaction.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 90 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 90 x g for 8-10 minutes at room temperature. The cell pellets will not be packed tightly, so care is required when removing the supernatant. 00:08:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet in 5...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet in 5 μl Nucleofector TM Solution per reaction. Work quickly, but carefully, and avoid leaving cells in the Nucleofector TM Solution for longer than 15 minutes. Avoid bubble formation. undetermined, Nucleofector Solution

steps.title: Add 5 μl of cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Add 5 μl of cell suspension to 25 μl of RNP solution to make 30 μl of cell-RNP solution per reaction. undetermined, cell suspension undetermined, RNP solution

steps.title: For each reaction, transfer all 30...
steps.instruction: For each reaction, transfer all 30 μl of cell-RNP solution to a well of the Nucleocuvette TM strip and click the lid into place.

steps.title: Gently tap the Nucleocuvette TM strip...
steps.instruction: Gently tap the Nucleocuvette TM strip on the benchtop to make sure that each sample covers the bottom of each well and that there are no bubbles in the wells.

steps.title: Pre-program the Nucleofector TM depending on...
steps.instruction: Pre-program the Nucleofector TM depending on the cell type per reaction. Make sure that the entire Nucleofector Supplement is added to the Nucleofector Solution (according to manufacturer's protocol) and that the mixture is not more than 3 months old.

steps.title: Place the Nucleocuvette TM strip with...
steps.instruction: Place the Nucleocuvette TM strip with closed lid into the retainer of the 4D-X Core unit. Check for proper orientation of the Nucleocuvette TM strip. Larger cutout is the top (A1 and A2) and smaller cutout is the bottom (H1 and H2).

steps.title: Press “Start” on the display of the core unit
steps.instruction: Press “Start” on the display of the core unit. After run completion, the screen should display a green “+” over the wells that were successfully transfected. Remove the cuvette strips from the Core unit. Some cell types require a 10-minute incubation at room temperature after nucleofection. Please consult the optimized Lonza protocol to see if this is a necessary step for your cell line.

steps.title: Carefully resuspend the cells in each...
steps.instruction: Carefully resuspend the cells in each well of the Nucleocuvette TM strip with 70 μl of pre-warmed growth medium, and mix gently by pipetting up and down 2-3 times. undetermined, pre-warmed growth medium

steps.title: Transfer all 100 μl to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer all 100 μl to the pre-warmed 12-well tissue culture plate (prepared in step 2)

steps.title: Incubate the cells for 2-3 days...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cells for 2-3 days in a humidified 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator. Undetermined, Incubation 48:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Conduct analyses to determine editing efficiency
steps.instruction: Conduct analyses to determine editing efficiency: PCR, Sanger sequencing, and ICE analysis . Next-Gen Sequencing, FACS, or functional tests may be conducted as alternatives. Option: If storing cells for future use is desired, split cells into two groups (one for analysis and one for cell culture).

steps.title: Extract DNA from cells 48 hours after transfection
steps.instruction: Extract DNA from cells 48 hours after transfection.

steps.title: Incubate RNPs for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate RNPs for 10 minutes at room temperature.
